name: Kentucky Legal Aid, Incorporated
description_short: Kentucky Legal Aid provides free civil legal services to low-income, disabled, and elderly clients across 35 counties in south-central and western Kentucky since 1977.
description_long:
Kentucky Legal Aid, Incorporated has served low-income, disabled, and senior citizens across south-central and western Kentucky since 1977. As the only free civil legal services provider in south-western Kentucky, the organization operates with fully certified legal professionals dedicated to serving clients in genuine legal crises. The organization maintains an active scam alert on its website, warning the public that all KLA services are genuinely free and directing people to report impostors.

The organization provides free legal advice and representation across multiple practice areas. Their service offerings include assistance for victims of domestic abuse and interpersonal violence, benefits counseling related to Medicare, Medicaid, SSI, and food stamps, housing counseling and crisis assistance for homeowners and renters, select consumer and family law cases, and other civil legal matters that threaten self-sufficiency or quality of life. All services are provided without charge to eligible clients.

Kentucky Legal Aid distinguishes itself through its geographic focus and specialized client populations. The organization serves 35 specific counties in Kentucky (Allen, Ballard, Barren, Butler, Caldwell, Calloway, Carlisle, Christian, Crittenden, Daviess, Edmonson, Fulton, Graves, Green, Hancock, Hart, Henderson, Hickman, Hopkins, Livingston, Logan, Lyon, Marshall, Metcalfe, McCracken, McLean, Muhlenberg, Ohio, Simpson, Taylor, Todd, Trigg, Union, Warren, and Webster), explicitly targeting individuals living in poverty and vulnerable populations. The organization also actively encourages community engagement through volunteer and donor opportunities to help close the justice gap.

As a legitimate nonprofit legal aid organization, Kentucky Legal Aid is a genuine resource for qualifying residents facing civil legal crises. The primary limitation is strict geographic eligibility—services only cover the designated 35-county service area in western and south-central Kentucky. Prospective clients must meet income and other eligibility requirements, and the organization focuses specifically on civil (not criminal) matters.
pros: Completely free legal services with no hidden fees or charges
Serves 35 counties across south-central and western Kentucky
Operates since 1977 with fully certified legal professionals
Specializes in vulnerable populations including elderly, disabled, and low-income clients
Addresses critical legal areas including domestic violence, housing crisis, and benefits issues
Actively combats scams with prominent fraud alerts on website
Accepts volunteer and donor involvement to support the justice gap mission
cons: Geographic service area limited to 35 specific Kentucky counties only
Eligibility likely requires income qualification and other criteria beyond stated information
Focuses on civil matters only—does not provide criminal legal representation
May have capacity limitations resulting in waitlists or limited availability
Website does not clearly detail specific eligibility requirements or application process details
best_for: Low-income residents in south-central or western Kentucky facing civil legal crises
Victims of domestic abuse and interpersonal violence in the service area
Senior citizens and disabled individuals with benefits or housing issues
Homeowners and renters in housing crisis situations within the 35-county region
diagnosis: Kentucky Legal Aid is the appropriate choice for low-income, elderly, or disabled residents in south-central or western Kentucky (35-county service area) facing civil legal crises that threaten their self-sufficiency or quality of life. The critical caveat is strict geographic limitation—the organization only serves designated counties in Kentucky, and prospective clients must meet income and eligibility requirements to qualify for free representation.
services: Free legal representation in civil matters
Domestic abuse and interpersonal violence assistance
SSI and food stamps benefits assistance
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 401 W 9Th St Ste D, Hopkinsville, KY 42240
